12.28: After seeking advice, the king made two golden calves and said to the people \to them/, “Going up to Jerusalem (city of peace) is too much for you?. Here, O Israel (he wrestles with God), are your gods, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage).
12.29: One calf he set up in Bethel (house of God), and the other in Dan (He has judged).
12.30: And this thing became a sin; the people walked as far as Dan to worship (He has judged) before one of the calves [the people walked to the one as far as Dan].
12.31: Jeroboam also built shrines on the high places and appointed from every class of people priests who were not Levites (joined to).
12.32: And Jeroboam (the people will contend) ordained a feast on the fifteenth day of the eighth month, like the feast that was in Judah (praised), and he offered sacrifices on the altar; he made (house of God) this offering in Bethel to sacrifice to the calves he had set up, and he installed priests in Bethel for the high places he had set up.
12.33: On the fifteenth day of the eighth month, a month of his own choosing, Jeroboam offered sacrifices on the altar he had set up in Bethel (house of God). So he ordained a feast for the Israelites (God prevails), offered sacrifices on the altar, and burned incense.

13.1: Suddenly, as Jeroboam (the people will contend) was standing beside the altar to burn incense, there came a man of God {Elohim} from Judah (praised) to Bethel (house of God) by the word of the LORD {YHWH}.
13.2: And he cried out against the altar by the word of the LORD {YHWH}, “O altar, O altar, this is what the LORD says: ‘A son named Josiah (whom YHWH heals) will be born to the house of David (beloved), and upon you he will sacrifice the priests of the high places who burn incense upon you, and human bones will be burned upon you <2Ki 23:16>.
13.3: That day the man of God gave a sign, saying, “The LORD {YHWH} has spoken this sign: ‘Surely the altar will be split apart, and the ashes upon it will be poured out.
13.4: Now when King Jeroboam (the people will contend), who was at the altar in Bethel (house of God), heard the word that the man of God {Elohim} had cried out against it, he stretched out his hand and said, “Seize him! But the hand he stretched out toward him withered, so that he could not pull it back.
13.5: And the altar was split apart, and the ashes poured out, according to the sign that the man of God {Elohim} had given by the word of the LORD {YHWH}.
13.6: Then the king responded to the man of God {Elohim}, “Intercede with the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} and pray for me that my hand may be restored. So the man of God interceded with the LORD, and the king’s hand was restored to him as it was before.
13.7: Then the king said to the man of God {Elohim}, “Come home with me and refresh yourself, and I will give you a reward.
13.8: But the man of God {Elohim} replied, “If you were to give me half your possessions, I still would not go with you, nor would I eat bread or drink water in this place.
13.9: For this is what I was commanded by the word of the LORD {YHWH}: ‘You must not eat bread or drink water or return by the way you came.
13.10: So the man of God went another way and did not return by the way he had come to Bethel (house of God).
13.11: Now a certain old prophet was living in Bethel (house of God), and his sons [son] came and told him all the deeds that the man of God {Elohim} had done that day in Bethel. They also told their father the words that the man had spoken to the king.
13.12: “Which way did he go? their father asked. And his sons showed him [had seen] the way taken by the man of God {Elohim}, who had come from Judah (praised).
13.13: So the prophet said to his sons, “Saddle the donkey for me. Then they saddled the donkey for him, and he mounted it
13.14: and went after the man of God {Elohim}. He found him sitting under an oak tree (a terebinth) and asked, “Are you the man of God who came from Judah (praised)? “I am, he replied.
13.15: So the prophet said to the man of God, “Come home with me and eat some bread.
13.16: But the man replied, “I cannot go home with you, and I will not eat bread or drink water with you in this place.
13.17: For I have been told by the word of the LORD {YHWH}: ‘You must not eat bread or drink water there or return by the way you came.
13.18: Then the prophet replied, “I too am a prophet like you, and an angel spoke to me by the word of the LORD {YHWH}, saying, ‘Bring him back with you to your house, so that he may eat bread and drink water. The old prophet was lying to him,
13.19: but the man of God went back with him, ate bread in his house, and drank water.
13.20: While they were sitting at the table, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to the prophet who had brought him back,
13.21: and the prophet cried out to the man of God {Elohim} who had come from Judah (praised), “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘Because you have defied the word of the LORD and have not kept the commandment that the LORD your God gave you,
13.22: but you went back and ate bread and drank water in the place where He told you not to do so, your body shall never reach the tomb of your fathers.
13.23: And after the man of God had finished eating and drinking, the old prophet who had brought him back saddled the donkey for him.
13.24: As he went on his way, a lion met him on the road and killed him, and his body was left lying in the road, with the donkey and the lion standing beside it.
13.25: And there were men passing by who saw the body lying in the road with the lion standing beside it, and they went and reported this in the city where the old prophet lived.
13.26: When the prophet who had brought him back from his journey heard this, he said, “It is the man of God {Elohim} who disobeyed the command of the LORD {YHWH}. Therefore the LORD has delivered him to the lion, and it has mauled him and killed him, according to the word that the LORD had spoken to him.
13.27: Then the old prophet instructed his sons, “Saddle the donkey for me. So they saddled it,
13.28: and he went and found the body lying in the road, with the donkey and the lion standing beside it. The lion had not eaten the body or mauled the donkey.
13.29: So the old prophet lifted up the body of the man of God {Elohim}, laid it on the donkey, and brought it back to his own city to mourn for him and bury him.
13.30: Then he laid the body in his own tomb, and they lamented over him, “Oh, my brother!
13.31: After he had buried him, the prophet said to his sons, “When I die, you? must bury me in the tomb where the man of God {Elohim} is buried. Lay my bones beside his bones,
13.32: for the message that he cried out by the word of the LORD {YHWH} against the altar in Bethel (house of God) and against all the shrines on the high places in the cities of Samaria (watch mountain) will surely come to pass.
13.33: Even after these events, Jeroboam (the people will contend) did not repent of his evil ways, but again he appointed priests for the high places from every class of people. He ordained anyone who desired to be a priest of the high places.
13.34: And this was the sin of the house of Jeroboam (the people will contend) that led to its extermination and destruction from the face of the earth.

14.1: At that time Abijah (YHWH is my father) son of Jeroboam (the people will contend) became ill,
14.2: and Jeroboam (the people will contend) said to his wife, “Now get up, disguise yourself so they will not recognize you as my wife, and go to Shiloh (place of rest). For Ahijah (brother of Yah) the prophet is there; it was he who spoke about my kingship over this people.
14.3: Take with you ten loaves of bread, some cakes, and a jar of honey, and go to him. He will tell you what will become of the boy.
14.4: Jeroboam’s (the people will contend) wife did as instructed; she arose and went to Shiloh (place of rest) and arrived at Ahijah’s (brother of Yah) house. Now Ahijah could not see, for his eyes were dim because of his age.
14.5: But the LORD {YHWH} had said to Ahijah (brother of Yah), “Behold, the wife of Jeroboam (the people will contend) is coming to ask you about her son, for he is ill. You are to say such and such to her, because when she arrives, she will be disguised.
14.6: So when Ahijah (brother of Yah) heard the sound of her feet entering the door, he said, “Come in, wife of Jeroboam (the people will contend)! Why are you disguised? For I have been sent to you with bad news.
14.7: Go, tell Jeroboam (the people will contend) that this is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘I raised you up from among the people and appointed you ruler over My people Israel.
14.8: I tore the kingdom away from the house of David (beloved) and gave it to you. But you have not been like My servant David, who kept My commandments and followed Me with all his heart, doing only what was right in My eyes.
14.9: You have done more evil than all who came before you. You have proceeded to make for yourself other gods and molten images to provoke Me, and you have flung Me behind your back.
14.10: Because of all this, behold, I am bringing disaster on the house of Jeroboam (the people will contend): I will cut off from Jeroboam every male \those who urinate against a wall/, both slave and free, in Israel (he wrestles with God); I will burn up the house of Jeroboam as one burns up dung until it is gone!
14.11: Anyone belonging to Jeroboam (the people will contend) who dies in the city will be eaten by dogs, and anyone who dies in the field will be eaten by the birds of the air. For the LORD {YHWH} has spoken.
14.12: As for you, get up and go home. When your feet enter the city, the child will die.
14.13: All Israel (he wrestles with God) will mourn for him and bury him. For this is the only one belonging to Jeroboam (the people will contend) who will receive a proper burial \who will come to the grave/, because only in him has the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, found any good in the house of Jeroboam.
14.14: Moreover, the LORD {YHWH} will raise up for Himself a king over Israel (he wrestles with God) who will cut off the house of Jeroboam (the people will contend). This is the day—yes, even today!
14.15: For the LORD {YHWH} will strike Israel (he wrestles with God) as a reed is shaken in the water. He will uproot Israel from this good land that He gave their fathers, and He will scatter them beyond the Euphrates \the River/, because they have made their Asherah poles (goddess), provoking the LORD to anger.
14.16: So He will give Israel over (he wrestles with God) on account of the sins Jeroboam (the people will contend) has committed and has caused Israel to commit.
14.17: Then Jeroboam’s (the people will contend) wife got up and departed for Tirzah (favorable), and as soon as she stepped over the threshold of the house, the boy died.
14.18: And they buried him, and all Israel (he wrestles with God) mourned for him, according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken through His servant Ahijah (brother of Yah) the prophet.
14.19: As for the rest of the acts of Jeroboam (the people will contend), how he waged war and how he reigned, they are indeed written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God).
14.20: And the length of Jeroboam’s (the people will contend) reign was twenty-two years, and he rested with his fathers, and his son Nadab (generous) reigned in his place.
14.21: Meanwhile, Rehoboam (a people has enlarged) son of Solomon (peace) reigned in Judah (praised). He was forty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem (city of peace), the city the LORD {YHWH} had chosen from all the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God) in which to put His Name. His mother’s name was Naamah (loveliness) the Ammonite.
14.22: And Judah (praised) did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}, and by the sins they committed they provoked Him to jealous anger more than all their fathers had done.
14.23: They also built for themselves high places, sacred pillars, and Asherah poles (goddess) on every high hill and under every green tree.
14.24: There were even male shrine prostitutes in the land. They imitated all the abominations of the nations the LORD {YHWH} had driven out before the Israelites (God prevails).
14.25: In the fifth year of Rehoboam’s (a people has enlarged) reign, Shishak (greedy of fine linen) king of Egypt (land of bondage) attacked Jerusalem (city of peace).
14.26: He seized the treasures of the house of the LORD {YHWH} and of the royal palace. He took everything, including all the gold shields that Solomon (peace) had made.
14.27: Then King Rehoboam (a people has enlarged) made bronze shields in their place and committed them to the care of the captains of the guard on duty at the entrance to the royal palace.
14.28: And whenever the king entered the house of the LORD {YHWH}, the guards would bear the shields, and later they would return them to the guardroom.
14.29: As for the rest of the acts of Rehoboam (a people has enlarged), along with all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah (praised)?
14.30: There was war between Rehoboam (a people has enlarged) and Jeroboam (the people will contend) throughout their days.
14.31: And Rehoboam (a people has enlarged) rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the City of David (beloved); his mother’s name was Naamah (loveliness) the Ammonite. And his son Abijam [Abijah] reigned in his place.

15.1: In the eighteenth year of the reign of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect), Abijam [Abijah] became king of Judah (praised),
15.2: and he reigned in Jerusalem (city of peace) three years. His mother’s name was Maacah (oppression) daughter of Abishalom (Absalom).
15.3: And Abijam walked in all the sins that his father before him had committed, and his heart was not as fully devoted to the LORD his God {YHWH Elohaw} as the heart of David (beloved) his forefather had been.
15.4: Nevertheless, for the sake of David (beloved), the LORD his God {YHWH Elohaw} gave him a lamp in Jerusalem (city of peace) by raising up a son to succeed him and to make Jerusalem strong.
15.5: For David (beloved) had done what was right in the eyes of the LORD {YHWH} and had not turned aside from anything the LORD commanded all the days of his life, except in the matter of Uriah (YHWH is my light) the Hittite.
15.6: And there was war between the houses of Rehoboam and Jeroboam (the people will contend) all the days of Abijam’s life \of his life/.
15.7: As for the rest of the acts of Abijam (my father is the sea), along with all his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah (praised)? And there was war between Abijam and Jeroboam (the people will contend).
15.8: And Abijam (my father is the sea) rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David (beloved), and his son Asa (healer) reigned in his place.
15.9: In the twentieth year of Jeroboam’s (the people will contend) reign over Israel (he wrestles with God), Asa (healer) became king of Judah (praised),
15.10: and he reigned in Jerusalem (city of peace) forty-one years. His grandmother’s (The queen mother’s) name was Maacah (oppression) daughter of Abishalom (my father is peace).
15.11: And Asa (healer) did what was right in the eyes of the LORD {YHWH}, as his father David had done (beloved).
15.12: He banished the male shrine prostitutes from the land and removed all the idols that his fathers had made.
15.13: He also removed his grandmother Maacah (oppression) from her position as queen mother because she had made a detestable Asherah pole (goddess). Asa (healer) chopped down the pole and burned it in the Kidron (dark) Valley.
15.14: The high places were not removed, but Asa’s (healer) heart was fully devoted to the LORD {YHWH} all his days.
15.15: And he brought into the house of the LORD {YHWH} the silver and gold and the articles that he and his father had dedicated.
15.16: Now there was war between Asa (healer) and Baasha (wicked) king of Israel (he wrestles with God) throughout their days.
15.17: Baasha (wicked) king of Israel (he wrestles with God) went to war against Judah (praised) and fortified Ramah (hill) to prevent anyone from leaving or entering the territory of Asa (healer) king of Judah.
15.18: So Asa (healer) withdrew all the silver and gold that remained in the treasuries of the house of the LORD {YHWH} and the royal palace. He entrusted it to his servants and sent them with this message to Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) son of Tabrimmon (good is Rimmon), the son of Hezion (vision), the king of Aram (exalted), who was ruling in Damascus (well-watered land):
15.19: “Let there be a treaty (covenant) between me and you as there was between my father and your father. See, I have sent you a gift of silver and gold. Now go and break your treaty with Baasha (wicked) king of Israel (he wrestles with God), so that he will withdraw from me.
15.20: And Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) listened to King Asa (healer) and sent the commanders of his armies against the cities of Israel (he wrestles with God), conquering Ijon (a ruin), Dan (He has judged), Abel-beth-maacah (house of pressure), and the whole land of Naphtali (wrestling), including the region of Chinnereth (harps).
15.21: When Baasha (wicked) learned of this, he stopped fortifying Ramah (hill) and withdrew to Tirzah (favorable).
15.22: Then King Asa (healer) summoned all the men of Judah, with no exceptions, and they carried away the stones of Ramah (hill) and the timbers Baasha (wicked) had used for building. And with these materials King Asa built up Geba of Benjamin (son of the right hand), as well as Mizpah (watchtower).
15.23: Now the rest of the acts of Asa (healer), along with all his might, all his accomplishments, and the cities he built, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah (praised)? In his old age, however, he became diseased in his feet.
15.24: And Asa (healer) rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the city of his father David (beloved), and his son Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) reigned in his place.
15.25: In the second year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Nadab (generous) son of Jeroboam (the people will contend) became king of Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned two years.
15.26: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH} and walked in the way of his father and in his sin, which he had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit.
15.27: Then Baasha (wicked) son of Ahijah (brother of Yah) of the house of Issachar (there is recompense) conspired against Nadab, and Baasha struck him down at Gibbethon (mound) of the Philistines (immigrants) while Nadab (generous) and all Israel (he wrestles with God) were besieging the city.
15.28: In the third year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Baasha (wicked) killed Nadab and reigned in his place.
15.29: As soon as Baasha became king, he struck down the entire household of Jeroboam (the people will contend). He did not leave to Jeroboam anyone who breathed, but destroyed them all according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken through His servant Ahijah (brother of Yah) the Shilonite,
15.30: because of the sins Jeroboam (the people will contend) had committed and had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit, and because he had provoked the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, to anger.
15.31: As for the rest of the acts of Nadab (generous), along with all his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
15.32: And there was war between Asa (healer) and Baasha (wicked) king of Israel (he wrestles with God) throughout their days.
15.33: In the third year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Baasha (wicked) son of Ahijah (brother of Yah) became king of all Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned in Tirzah (favorable) twenty-four years.
15.34: And Baasha did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH} and walked in the way of Jeroboam (the people will contend) and in his sin, which he had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit.

16.1: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Jehu (YHWH is He) son of Hanani (gracious) against Baasha (wicked), saying:
16.2: “Even though I lifted you out of the dust and made you ruler over My people Israel (he wrestles with God), you have walked in the way of Jeroboam (the people will contend) and have caused My people Israel to sin and to provoke Me to anger by their sins.
16.3: So now I will consume Baasha (wicked) and his house, and I will make your house like that of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect):
16.4: Anyone belonging to Baasha (wicked) who dies in the city will be eaten by dogs, and anyone who dies in the field will be eaten by the birds of the air.
16.5: As for the rest of the acts of Baasha (wicked), along with his accomplishments and might, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
16.6: And Baasha (wicked) rested with his fathers and was buried in Tirzah (favorable), and his son Elah (An oak) reigned in his place.
16.7: Moreover, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came through the prophet Jehu (YHWH is He) son of Hanani (gracious) against Baasha (wicked) and his house, because of all the evil he had done in the sight of the LORD, provoking Him to anger with the work of his hands and becoming like the house of Jeroboam (the people will contend), and also because Baasha had struck down the house of Jeroboam.
16.8: In the twenty-sixth year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Elah (An oak) son of Baasha (wicked) became king of Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned in Tirzah (favorable) two years.
16.9: However, while Elah was in Tirzah (favorable) getting drunk in the house of Arza (earthy) the steward of his household there, Elah’s servant Zimri (my music), the commander of half his chariots, conspired against him.
16.10: So in the twenty-seventh year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Zimri (my music) went in, struck Elah down, and killed him. And Zimri reigned in his place.
16.11: As soon as Zimri began to reign and was seated on the throne, he struck down the entire household of Baasha (wicked). He did not leave a single male, whether a kinsman or friend.
16.12: So Zimri (my music) destroyed the entire household of Baasha (wicked), according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken against Baasha through Jehu (YHWH is He) the prophet.
16.13: This happened because of all the sins Baasha (wicked) and his son Elah (An oak) had committed and had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit, provoking the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, to anger with their worthless idols.
16.14: As for the rest of the acts of Elah (An oak), along with all his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
16.15: In the twenty-seventh year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Zimri (my music) reigned in Tirzah (favorable) for seven days. Now the troops were encamped against Gibbethon (mound) of the Philistines (immigrants),
16.16: and the people in the camp heard that Zimri (my music) had conspired against the king and struck him down. So there in the camp that very day, all Israel (he wrestles with God) proclaimed Omri (pupil of YHWH), the commander of the army, king over Israel.
16.17: Then Omri (pupil of YHWH) and all the Israelites (God prevails) marched up from Gibbethon (mound) and besieged Tirzah (favorable).
16.18: When Zimri (my music) saw that the city was captured, he entered the citadel of the royal palace and burned it down upon himself. So he died
16.19: because of the sins he had committed, doing evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH} and following the example of Jeroboam (the people will contend) and the sin he had committed and had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit.
16.20: As for the rest of the acts of Zimri (my music) and the treason he committed, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
16.21: At that time the people of Israel (he wrestles with God) were divided: Half of the people supported Tibni (intelligent) son of Ginath (protection) as king, and half supported Omri (pupil of YHWH).
16.22: But the followers of Omri (pupil of YHWH) proved stronger than those of Tibni (intelligent) son of Ginath (protection). So Tibni died and Omri became king.
16.23: In the thirty-first year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Omri (pupil of YHWH) became king of Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned twelve years, six of them in Tirzah (favorable).
16.24: He bought the hill of Samaria (watch mountain) from Shemer (preserved) for two talents of silver <151 lb / 68.4 kg> and built a city there, calling it Samaria after the name of Shemer, who had owned the hill.
16.25: But Omri (pupil of YHWH) did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH} and acted more wickedly than all who were before him.
16.26: For he walked in all the ways of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect) and in his sins, which he caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to commit, provoking the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, to anger with their worthless idols.
16.27: As for the rest of the acts of Omri (pupil of YHWH), along with his accomplishments and the might he exercised, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
16.28: And Omri (pupil of YHWH) rested with his fathers and was buried in Samaria (watch mountain), and his son Ahab (father's brother) reigned in his place.
16.29: In the thirty-eighth year of Asa’s (healer) reign over Judah (praised), Ahab (father's brother) son of Omri (pupil of YHWH) became king of Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned in Samaria (watch mountain) twenty-two years.
16.30: However, Ahab (father's brother) son of Omri (pupil of YHWH) did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}, more than all who were before him.
16.31: And as if it were not enough for him to walk in the sins of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect), he even married Jezebel (Baal exalts) the daughter of Ethbaal (with Baal) king of the Sidonians, and he then proceeded to serve (lord) and worship Baal.
16.32: First, Ahab set up an altar for Baal (lord) in the temple of Baal that he had built in Samaria (watch mountain).
16.33: Then he (father's brother) set up an Asherah pole (goddess). Thus Ahab did more to provoke the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, to anger (he wrestles with God) than all the kings of Israel before him.
16.34: In Ahab’s days, Hiel (God lives) the Bethelite (house of God) rebuilt Jericho (city of palms). At the cost of Abiram (my father is exalted) his firstborn he laid its foundation, and at the cost of Segub (exalted) his youngest he set up its gates, according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken through Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun .

17.1: Now Elijah (my God is YHWH) the Tishbite (captivity), who was among the settlers of Gilead (who was from Tishbe in Gilead), said to Ahab (father's brother), “As surely as the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), lives, before whom I stand, there will be neither dew nor rain in these years except at my word!
17.2: Then a revelation from the LORD {YHWH} came to Elijah:
17.3: “Leave here, turn eastward, and hide yourself by the Brook of Cherith (cutting), east of the Jordan (the Cherith Ravine, near the Jordan).
17.4: And you are to drink from the brook, and I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.
17.5: So Elijah did what the LORD {YHWH} had told him, and he went and lived by the Brook of Cherith (cutting), east of the Jordan (flowing down).
17.6: The ravens would bring him bread and meat in the morning and evening, and he would drink from the brook.
17.7: Some time later, however, the brook dried up because there had been no rain in the land.
17.8: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Elijah:
17.9: “Get up and go to Zarephath (refinery) of Sidon (hunting), and stay there. Behold, I have commanded a widow there to provide for you.
17.10: So Elijah got up and went to Zarephath (refinery). When he arrived at the city gate, there was a widow gathering sticks. Elijah called to her and said, “Please bring me a little water in a cup, so that I may drink.
17.11: And as she was going to get it, he called to her and said, “Please bring me a piece of bread.
17.12: But she replied, “As surely as the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} lives, I have no bread—only a handful of flour in a jar and a little oil in a jug. Look, I am gathering a couple of sticks to take home and prepare a meal for myself and my son, so that we may eat it and die.
17.13: “Do not be afraid, Elijah (my God is YHWH) said to her. “Go and do as you have said. But first make me a small cake of bread from what you have, and bring it out to me. Afterward, make some for yourself and your son,
17.14: for this is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘The jar of flour will not be exhausted and the jug of oil will not run dry until the day the LORD sends rain upon the face of the earth.
17.15: So she went and did according to the word of Elijah (my God is YHWH), and there was food every day for Elijah and the woman and her household.
17.16: The jar of flour was not exhausted and the jug of oil did not run dry, according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken through Elijah (my God is YHWH).
17.17: Later, the son of the woman who owned the house became ill, and his sickness grew worse and worse, until no breath remained in him.
17.18: “O man of God {Elohim}, said the woman to Elijah (my God is YHWH), “what have you done to me? Have you come to remind me of my iniquity and cause the death of my son?
17.19: But Elijah said to her, “Give me your son. So he took him from her arms, carried him to the upper room where he was staying, and laid him on his own bed.
17.20: Then he cried out to the LORD {YHWH}, “O LORD my God {YHWH Elohay}, have You also brought tragedy on this widow who has opened her home to me, by causing her son to die?
17.21: Then he stretched himself out over the child three times and cried out to the LORD {YHWH}, “O LORD my God {YHWH Elohay}, please let this boy’s life return to him!
17.22: And the LORD {YHWH} listened to the voice of Elijah (my God is YHWH), and the child’s life returned to him, and he lived.
17.23: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) took the child, brought him down from the upper room into the house, and gave him to his mother. “Look, your son is alive, Elijah declared.
17.24: Then the woman said to Elijah (my God is YHWH), “Now I know that you are a man of God {Elohim} and that the word of the LORD {YHWH} from your mouth is truth.

18.1: After a long time, in the third year of the drought \And the days were many, and in the third year,/, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Elijah (my God is YHWH): “Go and present yourself to Ahab (father's brother), and I will send rain upon the face of the earth.
18.2: So Elijah (my God is YHWH) went to present himself to Ahab (father's brother). The famine was severe in Samaria (watch mountain),
18.3: and Ahab (father's brother) summoned Obadiah (servant of YHWH), who was in charge of the palace. (Now Obadiah greatly feared the LORD {YHWH},
18.4: for when Jezebel (Baal exalts) had slaughtered the prophets of the LORD {YHWH}, Obadiah (servant of YHWH) had taken a hundred prophets and hidden them, fifty men per cave, providing them with food and water.
18.5: Then Ahab (father's brother) said to Obadiah (servant of YHWH), “Go throughout the land to every spring and every valley. Perhaps we will find grass to keep the horses and mules alive so that we will not have to destroy any livestock.
18.6: So they divided the land to explore. Ahab (father's brother) went one way by himself, and Obadiah (servant of YHWH) went the other way by himself.
18.7: Now as Obadiah (servant of YHWH) went on his way, Elijah (my God is YHWH) suddenly met him. When Obadiah recognized him, he fell facedown and said, “Is it you, my lord Elijah?
18.8: “It is I, he answered. “Go tell your master, ‘Elijah (my God is YHWH) is here!
18.9: But Obadiah replied, “How have I sinned, that you are handing your servant over to Ahab (father's brother) to put me to death?
18.10: As surely as the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} lives, there is no nation or kingdom where my lord has not sent someone to search for you. When they said, ‘He is not here, he made that kingdom or nation swear that they had not found you.
18.11: And now you say, ‘Go tell your master that Elijah (my God is YHWH) is here!
18.12: I do not know where the Spirit of the LORD {Ruach YHWH} may carry you off when I leave you. Then when I go and tell Ahab (father's brother) and he does not find you, he will kill me. But I, your servant, have feared the LORD from my youth.
18.13: Was it not reported to my lord what I did when Jezebel (Baal exalts) slaughtered the prophets of the LORD {YHWH}? I hid a hundred prophets of the LORD, fifty men per cave, and I provided them with food and water.
18.14: And now you say, ‘Go tell your lord that Elijah (my God is YHWH) is here! He will kill me!
18.15: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) said, “As surely as the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts lives, before whom I stand, I will present myself to Ahab today.
18.16: So Obadiah (servant of YHWH) went to inform Ahab (father's brother), who went to meet Elijah (my God is YHWH).
18.17: When Ahab (father's brother) saw Elijah (my God is YHWH), he said to him, “Is that you, O troubler of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
18.18: “I have not troubled Israel (he wrestles with God), Elijah replied, “but you and your father’s house have, for you? have forsaken the commandments of the LORD {YHWH} and have followed the Baals (lord).
18.19: Now summon all Israel (he wrestles with God) to meet me on Mount Carmel (garden-land), along with the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal (lord) and the four hundred prophets of Asherah (goddess) who eat at Jezebel’s (Baal exalts) table.
18.20: So Ahab (father's brother) summoned all the Israelites (God prevails) and assembled the prophets on Mount Carmel (garden-land).
18.21: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) approached all the people and said, “How long will you? waver between two opinions? If the LORD {YHWH} is God {Elohim}, follow Him. But if Baal is God (lord), follow him. But the people did not answer a word.
18.22: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) said to the people, “I am the only remaining prophet of the LORD {YHWH}, but Baal has (lord) four hundred and fifty prophets.
18.23: Get two bulls for us. Let the prophets of Baal choose one bull for themselves, cut it into pieces, and place it on the wood but not light the fire. And I will prepare the other bull and place it on the wood but not light the fire.
18.24: Then you? may call on the name of your? god, and I will call on the name of the LORD {YHWH}. The God {Elohim} who answers by fire, He is God. And all the people answered, “What you say is good.
18.25: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) said to the prophets of Baal (lord), “Since you? are so numerous, choose for yourselves one bull and prepare it first. Then call on the name of your? god, but do not light the fire.
18.26: And they took the bull that was given them, prepared it, and called on the name of Baal (lord) from morning until noon, shouting, “O Baal, answer us! But there was no sound, and no one answered as they leaped around the altar they had made.
18.27: At noon Elijah (my God is YHWH) began to taunt them, saying, “Shout louder, for he is a god {Elohim}! Perhaps he is deep in thought, or occupied, or on a journey. Perhaps he is sleeping and must be awakened!
18.28: So they shouted louder and cut themselves with knives and lances, as was their custom, until the blood gushed over them.
18.29: Midday passed, and they kept on raving until the time of the evening sacrifice. But there was no response; no one answered, no one paid attention.
18.30: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) said to all the people, “Come near to me. So all the people approached him, and he repaired the altar of the LORD {YHWH} that had been torn down.
18.31: And Elijah (my God is YHWH) took twelve stones, one for each tribe of the sons of Jacob (he grasps the heel), to whom the word of the LORD {YHWH} had come and said, “Israel (he wrestles with God) shall be your name.
18.32: And with the stones, Elijah built an altar in the name of the LORD {YHWH}. Then he dug a trench around the altar large enough to hold two seahs of seed <13.2 qt / 14.6 l>.
18.33: Next, he arranged the wood, cut up the bull, placed it on the wood,
18.34: and said, “Fill four waterpots and pour the water on the offering and on the wood. “Do it a second time, he said, and they did it a second time. “Do it a third time, he said, and they did it a third time.
18.35: So the water ran down around the altar and even filled the trench.
18.36: At the time of the evening sacrifice, Elijah (my God is YHWH) the prophet approached the altar and said, “O LORD, God {YHWH Elohe} of Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he laughs), and Israel (he wrestles with God), let it be known this day that You are God in Israel and that I am Your servant and have done all these things at Your command.
18.37: Answer me, O LORD {YHWH}! Answer me, so that this people will know that You, the LORD, are God {Elohim}, and that You have turned their hearts back again.
18.38: Then the fire of the LORD {YHWH} fell and consumed the sacrifice, the wood, the stones, and the dust, and it licked up the water in the trench.
18.39: When all the people saw this, they fell facedown and said, “The LORD {YHWH}, He is God {Elohim}! The LORD, He is God!
18.40: Then Elijah (my God is YHWH) ordered them, “Seize the prophets of Baal (lord)! Do not let a single one escape. So they seized them, and Elijah brought them down to the Kishon (winding) Valley and slaughtered them there.
18.41: And Elijah (my God is YHWH) said to Ahab (father's brother), “Go up, eat and drink, for there is the sound of a heavy rain.
18.42: So Ahab (father's brother) went up to eat and drink. But Elijah (my God is YHWH) climbed to the summit of Carmel (garden-land), bent down on the ground, and put his face between his knees.
18.43: “Go and look toward the sea, he said to his servant. So the servant went and looked, and he said, “There is nothing there. Seven times Elijah said, “Go back.
18.44: On the seventh time the servant reported, “There is a cloud as small as a man’s hand rising from the sea. And Elijah replied, “Go and tell Ahab (father's brother), ‘Prepare your chariot and go down before the rain stops you.
18.45: Meanwhile, the sky grew dark with clouds and wind {ruach}, and a heavy rain began to fall. So Ahab (father's brother) rode away and went to Jezreel (God sows).
18.46: And the hand of the LORD {YHWH} came upon Elijah (my God is YHWH), and he tucked his cloak into his belt \he girded up his loins/ and ran ahead of Ahab (father's brother) all the way to Jezreel (God sows).

19.1: Now Ahab (father's brother) told Jezebel (Baal exalts) everything that Elijah (my God is YHWH) had done and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.
19.2: So Jezebel (Baal exalts) sent a messenger to Elijah (my God is YHWH), saying, “May the gods deal with me, and ever so severely, if by this time tomorrow I have not made your life like the lives of those you killed!
19.3: And Elijah was afraid and ran for his life. When he came to Beersheba (well of seven) in Judah (praised), he left his servant there,
19.4: while he himself went a day’s journey into the wilderness. He sat down under a broom tree and prayed that he might die. “I have had enough, LORD {YHWH}, he said. “Take my life, for I am no better than my fathers.
19.5: Then he lay down under the broom tree and fell asleep. Suddenly an angel touched him and said, “Get up and eat.
19.6: And he looked around, and there by his head was a cake of bread baked over hot coals, and a jar of water. So he ate and drank and lay down again.
19.7: A second time the angel of the LORD {YHWH} returned and touched him, saying, “Get up and eat, or the journey will be too much for you.
19.8: So he got up and ate and drank. And strengthened by that food, he walked forty days and forty nights until he reached Horeb (Mount Sinai), the mountain of God {Elohim}.
19.9: There Elijah entered a cave and spent the night. And the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to him, saying, “What are you doing here, Elijah (my God is YHWH)?
19.10: “I have been very zealous for the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Hosts, he replied, “but the Israelites (God prevails) have forsaken Your covenant, torn down Your altars, and killed Your prophets with the sword. I am the only one left, and they are seeking my life as well .
19.11: Then the LORD said, “Go out and stand on the mountain before the LORD {YHWH}. Behold, the LORD is about to pass by. And a great and mighty wind {ruach} tore into the mountains and shattered the rocks before the LORD, but the LORD was not in the wind. After the wind there was an earthquake, but the LORD was not in the earthquake.
19.12: After the earthquake there was a fire, but the LORD {YHWH} was not in the fire. And after the fire came a still, small voice.
19.13: When Elijah (my God is YHWH) heard it, he wrapped his face in his cloak and went out and stood at the mouth of the cave. Suddenly a voice came to him and said, “What are you doing here, Elijah?
19.14: “I have been very zealous for the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Hosts, he replied, “but the Israelites (God prevails) have forsaken Your covenant, torn down Your altars, and killed Your prophets with the sword. I am the only one left, and they are seeking my life as well.
19.15: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to him, “Go back by the way you came, and go to the Desert of Damascus (well-watered land). When you arrive, you are to anoint Hazael (one who sees God) as king over Aram (exalted).
19.16: You are also to anoint Jehu (YHWH is He) son (grandson) of Nimshi (rescued) as king over Israel (he wrestles with God) and Elisha (God is salvation) son of Shaphat (He has judged) from Abel-meholah (meadow of dancing) to succeed you as prophet.
19.17: Then Jehu (YHWH is He) will put to death whoever escapes the sword of Hazael (one who sees God), and Elisha (God is salvation) will put to death whoever escapes the sword of Jehu.
19.18: Nevertheless, I have reserved seven thousand in Israel (he wrestles with God)—all whose knees have not bowed to Baal and whose mouths have not kissed him.
19.19: So Elijah departed and found Elisha (God is salvation) son of Shaphat (He has judged). He was plowing with twelve teams of oxen, and he was with the twelfth team. Elijah (my God is YHWH) passed by him and threw his cloak around him.
19.20: So Elisha left the oxen, ran after Elijah (my God is YHWH), and said, “Please let me kiss my father and mother goodbye, and then I will follow you. “Go on back, Elijah replied, “for what have I done to you?
19.21: So Elisha turned back from him, took his pair of oxen, and slaughtered them. Using the oxen’s equipment for fuel, he cooked the meat and gave it to the people, and they ate. Then he set out to follow and serve Elijah (my God is YHWH).

20.1: Now Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) king of Aram (exalted) assembled his entire army. Accompanied by thirty-two kings with their horses and chariots, he marched up, besieged Samaria (watch mountain), and waged war against it.
20.2: Then he sent messengers into the city to Ahab (father's brother) king of Israel (he wrestles with God),
20.3: saying, “This is what Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) says: ‘Your silver and gold are mine, and your best wives and children are mine!
20.4: And the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) replied, “Just as you say, my lord the king: I am yours, along with all that I have.
20.5: The messengers came back and said, “This is what Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) says: ‘I have sent to you to demand your silver, your gold, your wives, and your children.
20.6: But about this time tomorrow I will send my servants to search your palace and the houses of your servants. They will seize and carry away all that is precious to you.
20.7: Then the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) summoned all the elders of the land and said, “Please take note and see that this man is looking for trouble, for when he demanded my wives, my children, my silver, and my gold, I did not deny him.
20.8: And the elders and the people all said, “Do not listen to him or consent to his terms.
20.9: So Ahab answered the messengers of Ben-hadad (son of Hadad), “Tell my lord the king, ‘All that you demanded of your servant the first time I will do, but this thing I cannot do. So the messengers departed and relayed the message to Ben-hadad.
20.10: Then Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) sent another message to Ahab: “May the gods deal with me, and ever so severely, if enough dust remains of Samaria (watch mountain) for each of my men to have a handful.
20.11: And the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) replied, “Tell him: ‘The one putting on his armor should not boast like one taking it off.
20.12: Ben-hadad received this message while he and the kings were drinking in their tents (in Succoth), and he said to his servants, “Take your positions. So they stationed themselves against the city.
20.13: Meanwhile a prophet approached Ahab (father's brother) king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and declared, “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘Do you see this entire great army? Behold, I will deliver it into your hand this very day, and you will know that I am the LORD.
20.14: “By whom? Ahab (father's brother) asked. And the prophet replied, “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘By the young officers of the district governors. “Who will start the battle? asked Ahab. “You will, answered the prophet.
20.15: So Ahab assembled the young officers of the district governors, and there were 232 men. And after them, he assembled the rest of the Israelite (he wrestles with God) troops, 7,000 in all.
20.16: They marched out at noon while Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) and the 32 kings allied with him were in their tents getting drunk.
20.17: And the young officers of the district governors marched out first. Now Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) had sent out scouts, who reported to him, “Men are marching out of Samaria (watch mountain).
20.18: “If they have marched out in peace, he said, “take them alive. Even if they have marched out for war, take them alive.
20.19: Meanwhile, these young officers of the district governors marched out of the city, with the army behind them,
20.20: and each one struck down his opponent. So the Arameans (exalted) fled, with the Israelites (God prevails) in pursuit. But Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) king of Aram escaped on horseback with the cavalry.
20.21: Then the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) marched out and attacked the horses and chariots, inflicting a great slaughter on the Arameans (exalted).
20.22: Afterward, the prophet approached the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and said, “Go and strengthen your position, and take note what you must do, for in the spring \at the turn of the year/ the king of Aram (exalted) will come up against you.
20.23: Meanwhile, the servants of the king of Aram (exalted) said to him, “Their gods are gods of the hills. That is why they prevailed over us. Instead, we should fight them on the plains; surely then we will prevail.
20.24: So do this: Dismiss all the kings from their positions and replace them with other officers.
20.25: And you must raise an army like the one you have lost—horse for horse and chariot for chariot—so we can fight the Israelites on the plain, where we will surely prevail. And the king approved their plan and acted accordingly.
20.26: In the spring, Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) mobilized the Arameans (exalted) and went up to Aphek (enclosure) to fight against Israel (he wrestles with God).
20.27: The Israelites (God prevails) also mobilized, gathered supplies, and marched out to meet them. The Israelites camped before them like two small flocks of goats, while the Arameans (exalted) covered the countryside.
20.28: Then the man of God {Elohim} approached the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and said, “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘Because the Arameans (exalted) have said that the LORD is a god of the hills and not of the valleys, I will deliver all this great army into your hand. Then you? will know that I am the LORD.
20.29: For seven days the armies camped opposite each other, and on the seventh day the battle ensued, and the Israelites (God prevails) struck down the Arameans (exalted)—a hundred thousand foot soldiers in one day.
20.30: The rest of them fled into the city of Aphek (enclosure), where the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand of the remaining men. Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) also fled to the city and hid in an inner room.
20.31: Then the servants of Ben-hadad said to him, “Look now, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel (he wrestles with God) are merciful. Let us go out to the king of Israel with sackcloth around our waists and ropes around our heads. Perhaps he will spare your life.
20.32: So with sackcloth around their waists and ropes around their heads, they went to the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and said, “Your servant Ben-hadad (son of Hadad) says, ‘Please spare my life. And the king answered, “Is he still alive? He is my brother.
20.33: Now the men were looking for a sign of hope, and they quickly grasped at this word and replied, “Yes, your brother Ben-hadad (son of Hadad). “Go and get him! said the king. Then Ben-hadad came out, and Ahab had him come up into his chariot.
20.34: Ben-hadad said to him, “I will restore the cities my father took from your father; you may set up your own marketplaces in Damascus (well-watered land), as my father did in Samaria (watch mountain). “By this treaty (covenant) I release you,” Ahab replied. So he made a treaty with him and sent him away.
20.35: Meanwhile, by the word of the LORD {YHWH}, one of the sons of the prophets said to his companion, “Strike me, please! But the man refused to strike him.
20.36: Then the prophet said to him, “Because you have not obeyed the voice of the LORD {YHWH}, as soon as you depart from me a lion will kill you. And when he left, a lion found him and killed him.
20.37: Then the prophet found another man and said, “Strike me, please! So the man struck him and wounded him,
20.38: and the prophet went and waited on the road for the king, disguising himself with a bandage over his eyes.
20.39: As the king passed by, he cried out to the king: “Your servant had marched out into the middle of the battle, when suddenly a man came over with a captive and told me, ‘Guard this man! If he goes missing for any reason, your life will be exchanged for his life, or you will weigh out a talent of silver <75.4 lb / 34.2 kg>.
20.40: But while your servant was busy here and there, the man disappeared. And the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) said to him, “So shall your judgment be; you have pronounced it on yourself.
20.41: Then the prophet quickly removed the bandage from his eyes, and the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) recognized him as one of the prophets.
20.42: And the prophet said to the king, “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘Because you have let slip from your hand the man I had devoted to destruction {cherem}, your life will be exchanged for his life, and your people for his people.
20.43: Sullen and angry, the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) went home to Samaria (watch mountain).

21.1: Some time after these events, Naboth (fruits) the Jezreelite owned a vineyard in Jezreel (God sows) next to the palace of Ahab (father's brother) king of Samaria (watch mountain).
21.2: So Ahab (father's brother) said to Naboth (fruits), “Give me your vineyard to use as a vegetable garden, since it is next to my palace. I will give you a better vineyard in its place—or if you prefer, I will give you its value in silver.
21.3: But Naboth (fruits) replied (father's brother), “The LORD {YHWH} forbid that I should give you the inheritance of my fathers.
21.4: So Ahab (father's brother) went to his palace, sullen and angry because Naboth (fruits) the Jezreelite had told him, “I will not give you the inheritance of my fathers. He lay down on his bed, turned his face away, and refused to eat.
21.5: Soon his wife Jezebel (Baal exalts) came in and asked, “Why are you so sullen that you refuse to eat?
21.6: Ahab answered, “Because I spoke to Naboth (fruits) the Jezreelite and told him, ‘Give me your vineyard for silver, or if you wish, I will give you another vineyard in its place. And he replied, ‘I will not give you my vineyard!
21.7: But his wife Jezebel (Baal exalts) said to him, “Do you not reign over Israel (he wrestles with God)? Get up, eat some food, and be cheerful, for I will get you the vineyard of Naboth (fruits) the Jezreelite.
21.8: Then Jezebel wrote letters in Ahab’s (father's brother) name, sealed them with his seal, and sent them to the elders and nobles who lived with Naboth (fruits) in his city.
21.9: In the letters she wrote: “Proclaim a fast and give Naboth (fruits) a seat of honor among the people.
21.10: But seat two scoundrels opposite him and have them testify, ‘You have cursed both God {Elohim} and the king! Then take him out and stone him to death.
21.11: So the elders and nobles who lived in Naboth’s city did as Jezebel (Baal exalts) had instructed in the letters she had written to them.
21.12: They proclaimed a fast and gave Naboth (fruits) a seat of honor among the people.
21.13: And the two scoundrels came in and sat opposite Naboth, and these men testified against him (fruits) before the people, saying, “Naboth has cursed both God {Elohim} and the king! So they took him outside the city and stoned him to death.
21.14: Then they sent word to Jezebel (Baal exalts): “Naboth (fruits) has been stoned to death.
21.15: When Jezebel (Baal exalts) heard that Naboth (fruits) had been stoned to death, she said to Ahab (father's brother), “Get up and take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, who refused to give it to you for silver. For Naboth is no longer alive, but dead.
21.16: And when Ahab (father's brother) heard that Naboth (fruits) was dead, he got up and went down to take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite.
21.17: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Elijah (my God is YHWH) the Tishbite (captivity), saying,
21.18: “Get up and go down to meet Ahab (father's brother) king of Israel (he wrestles with God), who is in Samaria (watch mountain). See, he is in the vineyard of Naboth (fruits), where he has gone to take possession of it.
21.19: Tell him that this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘Have you not murdered a man and seized his land? Then tell him that this is also what the LORD says: ‘In the place where the dogs licked up the blood of Naboth (fruits), there also the dogs will lick up your blood yes, yours <1Ki 22:38>!
21.20: When Elijah arrived (my God is YHWH), Ahab (father's brother) said to him, “So you have found me out, my enemy. He replied, “I have found you out because you have sold yourself to do evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}.
21.21: This is what the LORD says [Behold]: ‘I will bring calamity on you and consume your descendants; I will cut off from Ahab (father's brother) every male in Israel (he wrestles with God), both slave and free.
21.22: I will make your house like that of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect) and like that of Baasha (wicked) son of Ahijah (brother of Yah), because you have provoked My anger and caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to sin.
21.23: And the LORD {YHWH} also speaks concerning Jezebel (Baal exalts): ‘The dogs will devour Jezebel by the wall of Jezreel.
21.24: Anyone belonging to Ahab (father's brother) who dies in the city will be eaten by dogs, and anyone who dies in the field will be eaten by the birds of the air.
21.25: (Surely there was never one like Ahab (father's brother), who sold himself to do evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}, incited by his wife Jezebel (Baal exalts).
21.26: He committed the most detestable acts by going after idols, just like the Amorites (sayers) whom the LORD {YHWH} had driven out before the Israelites (God prevails).
21.27: When Ahab (father's brother) heard these words, he tore his clothes, put on sackcloth, and fasted. He lay down in sackcloth and walked around meekly.
21.28: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Elijah (my God is YHWH) the Tishbite (captivity), saying:
21.29: “Have you seen how Ahab (father's brother) has humbled himself before Me? Because he has humbled himself before Me, I will not bring the calamity during his days, but I will bring it upon his house in the days of his son.

22.1: Then three years passed without war between Aram (exalted) and Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.2: However, in the third year, Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) king of Judah (praised) went down to visit the king of Israel (he wrestles with God),
22.3: who (he wrestles with God) said to his servants, “Do you? not know that Ramoth-gilead (heights) is ours, (rocky region) but we have failed to take it from the hand of the king of Aram (exalted)?
22.4: So he asked Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), “Will you go with me to fight against Ramoth-gilead (heights)? (rocky region) Jehoshaphat answered the king of Israel (he wrestles with God), “I am as you are, my people are your people, and my horses are your horses.
22.5: But Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) also said to the king of Israel (he wrestles with God), “Please inquire first for the word of the LORD {YHWH}.
22.6: So the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) assembled the prophets, about four hundred men, and asked them, “Should I go to war against Ramoth-gilead (heights) (rocky region), or should I refrain? “Go up, they replied, “and the Lord {Adonai} will deliver it into the hand of the king.
22.7: But Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) asked, “Is there not still a prophet of the LORD {YHWH} here of whom we can inquire?
22.8: The king of Israel (he wrestles with God) answered (YHWH has judged), “There is still one man through whom we can inquire of the LORD {YHWH}, but I hate him because he never prophesies anything good for me, but only bad. He is Micaiah (who is like God) son of Imlah (whom God will fill up). “The king should not say that! Jehoshaphat replied.
22.9: So the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) called one of his officials and said, “Bring Micaiah (who is like God) son of Imlah at once (whom God will fill up).
22.10: Dressed in royal attire, the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) king of Judah (praised) were sitting on their thrones at the threshing floor by the entrance of the gate of Samaria (watch mountain), with all the prophets prophesying before them.
22.11: Now Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) son of Chenaanah (trader) had made for himself iron horns and declared, “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘With these you shall gore the Arameans (exalted) until they are finished off.
22.12: And all the prophets were prophesying the same, saying, “Go up to Ramoth-gilead (heights) (rocky region) and triumph, for the LORD {YHWH} will deliver it into the hand of the king.
22.13: Then the messenger who had gone to call Micaiah (who is like God) instructed him, “Behold now, with one accord the words of the prophets are favorable to the king. So please let your words be like theirs, and speak favorably.
22.14: But Micaiah (who is like God) said, “As surely as the LORD {YHWH} lives, I will speak whatever the LORD tells me.
22.15: When Micaiah arrived, the king asked him, “Micaiah (who is like God), should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead (heights), (rocky region) or should we refrain? “Go up and triumph, Micaiah replied, “for the LORD {YHWH} will deliver it into the hand of the king.
22.16: But the king said to him, “How many times must I make you swear not to tell me anything but the truth in the name of the LORD {YHWH}?
22.17: So Micaiah declared: “I saw all Israel (he wrestles with God) scattered on the hills like sheep without a shepherd. And the LORD {YHWH} said, ‘These people have no master; let each one return home in peace.
22.18: Then the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) said to Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), “Did I not tell you that he never prophesies good for me, but only bad?
22.19: Micaiah continued, “Therefore hear the word of the LORD {YHWH}: I saw the LORD sitting on His throne, and all the host of heaven standing by Him on His right and on His left.
22.20: And the LORD {YHWH} said, ‘Who will entice Ahab (father's brother) to march up and fall at Ramoth-gilead (heights)? (rocky region) And one suggested this, and another that.
22.21: Then a spirit {ruach} came forward, stood before the LORD {YHWH}, and said, ‘I will entice him. ‘By what means? asked the LORD.
22.22: And he replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit {ruach} in the mouths of all his prophets. ‘You will surely entice him and prevail, said the LORD. ‘Go and do it.
22.23: So you see, the LORD {YHWH} has put a lying spirit {ruach} in the mouths of all these prophets of yours, and the LORD has pronounced disaster against you.
22.24: Then Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) son of Chenaanah (trader) went up, struck Micaiah (who is like God) in the face, and demanded, “Which way did the Spirit of the LORD {Ruach YHWH} go when He departed from me to speak with you?
22.25: Micaiah (who is like God) replied, “You will soon see, on that day when you go and hide in an inner room.
22.26: And the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) declared, “Take Micaiah (who is like God) and return him to Amon (skilled workman) the governor of the city and to Joash (given by the Lord) the king’s son,
22.27: and tell them that this is what the king says: ‘Put this man in prison and feed him only bread and water until I return safely.
22.28: But Micaiah (who is like God) replied, “If you ever return safely, the LORD {YHWH} has not spoken through me. Then he added, “Take heed, all you people!
22.29: So the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) and Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) king of Judah (praised) went up to Ramoth-gilead (heights) (rocky region).
22.30: And the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) said to Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your royal robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into battle.
22.31: Now the king of Aram (exalted) had ordered his thirty-two chariot commanders, “Do not fight with anyone, small or great, except the king of Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.32: When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), they said, “Surely this is the king of Israel (he wrestles with God)! So they turned to fight against him, but Jehoshaphat cried out.
22.33: And when the chariot commanders saw that he was not the king of Israel (he wrestles with God), they turned back from pursuing him.
22.34: However, a certain man drew his bow without taking special aim, and he struck the king of Israel (he wrestles with God) between the joints of his armor. So the king said to his charioteer, “Turn around \Turn your hand/ and take me out of the battle, for I am badly wounded!
22.35: The battle raged throughout that day, and the king was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans (exalted). And the blood from his wound ran out onto the floor of the chariot, and that evening he died.
22.36: As the sun was setting, the cry rang out in the army: “Every man to his own city, and every man to his own land!
22.37: So the king died and was brought to Samaria (watch mountain), where they buried him.
22.38: And the chariot was washed at the pool of Samaria (watch mountain) where the prostitutes bathed, and the dogs licked up Ahab’s blood, according to the word that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken <1Ki 21:19>.
22.39: As for the rest of the acts of Ahab (father's brother), along with all his accomplishments and the ivory palace and all the cities he built, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
22.40: And Ahab (father's brother) rested with his fathers, and his son Ahaziah (YHWH holds) reigned in his place.
22.41: In the fourth year of Ahab’s (father's brother) reign over Israel (he wrestles with God), Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) son of Asa (healer) became king of Judah (praised).
22.42: Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) was thirty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem (city of peace) twenty-five years. His mother’s name was Azubah (forsaken) daughter of Shilhi (armed).
22.43: And Jehoshaphat walked in all the ways of his father Asa (healer); he did not turn away from them, but did what was right in the eyes of the LORD {YHWH}. The high places, however, were not removed; the people still sacrificed and burned incense on the high places.
22.44: Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) also made peace with the king of Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.45: As for the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), along with the might he exercised and how he waged war, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah (praised)?
22.46: He banished from the land the male shrine prostitutes who remained from the days of his father Asa (healer).
22.47: And there was no king in Edom (red); a deputy served as king.
22.48: Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) built ships of Tarshish (a fleet of trading ships) to go to Ophir (reducing to ashes) for gold, but they never set sail, because they were wrecked at Ezion-geber (backbone).
22.49: At that time Ahaziah (YHWH holds) son of Ahab (father's brother) said to Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged), “Let my servants sail with your servants, but Jehoshaphat refused.
22.50: And Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the city of his father David (beloved). And his son Jehoram (YHWH is exalted) reigned in his place.
22.51: In the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat’s (YHWH has judged) reign over Judah (praised), Ahaziah (YHWH holds) son of Ahab (father's brother) became king of Israel (he wrestles with God), and he reigned in Samaria (watch mountain) two years.
22.52: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH} and walked in the ways of his father and mother and of Jeroboam (the people will contend) son of Nebat (aspect), who had caused Israel (he wrestles with God) to sin.
22.53: Ahaziah served and worshiped Baal (lord), provoking the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel, to anger (he wrestles with God), just as his father had done.
